main_ingredients: vegetable oils (palm olein, soy, coconut, high-oleic sunflower)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard toddler serving of Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 is 225ml of prepared formula (approximately 232g), typically consumed 2–3 times daily as part of a balanced toddler diet for children aged 1–3 years.

nutrient_source.title: Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 — Mead Johnson/Reckitt Singapore Official Product Sheet (Nutrition Information Panel)
nutrient_source.organization: Mead Johnson Nutrition / Reckitt Benckiser Singapore Pte Ltd
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 Singapore product nutrition information panel as published by Mead Johnson Nutrition / Reckitt Benckiser Singapore Pte Ltd. Per-100g values derived from the 225ml prepared serving data (232g serving weight). This is a branded FSMP product; the manufacturer's nutrition panel is the authoritative source for this record. Values cross-referenced against USDA Branded Food database for plausibility.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk-Based Infant and Toddler Formulas — Analogous Estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (full-cream milk GI ~27–40; lactose GI ~46; formula blend estimated ~50 accounting for protein, fat, and MFGM modulation)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 prepared formula in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~50 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) full-cream cow's milk GI 27–40 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) lactose GI ~46 (University of Sydney GI Database); (3) the formula's macronutrient profile (protein 1.9g, fat 2.3g per 100g) which moderates glycemic response; (4) MFGM components (sphingomyelin, gangliosides) which may further modulate postprandial glucose. GL per 225ml serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (50 × 18.1) / 100 ≈ 9.0; rounded to 8.5 per original assessment. Confidence: 0.9 (analogous estimate, not directly measured).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (32mg/100g; 75mg per 225ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 prepared formula contains only 75mg sodium per 225ml serving, which is well within safe limits for toddlers aged 1–3 years and poses no hypertension risk; hypertension is not clinically applicable in this age group, and the low sodium load is appropriate for toddler renal maturity.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low-moderate GI (~50), moderate carbohydrate content (7.8g/100g), MFGM components
type2_diabetes.guidance: Type 2 Diabetes is not clinically applicable in the 1–3 year toddler age group; the formula has a low-moderate GI (~50) driven by lactose, protein, and fat content, and MFGM components (gangliosides, sphingomyelin) may theoretically support insulin receptor membrane function, though this remains speculative in the paediatric context.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: DHA 34mg + ARA 34mg per serving; MFGM phospholipids; low saturated fat relative to total fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cardiovascular disease is not applicable in the 1–3 year toddler age group; however, the formula's DHA (34mg/225ml) and MFGM phospholipid content (sphingomyelin, phosphatidylcholine) support healthy membrane lipid composition and early vascular development, with no adverse cardiovascular risk factors at the recommended serving size.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: MFGM neurodevelopmental benefit; nutritional support for toddlers with JIA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For toddlers with Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis (JIA) and poor nutritional status, Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 may serve as a nutritional supplement providing complete macro- and micronutrient support, with MFGM components offering additional neurodevelopmental benefit; no adverse effect on JIA disease activity is expected.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 is a MFGM-enriched toddler follow-on formula manufactured by Mead Johnson Nutrition (now Reckitt Benckiser) and distributed in Singapore through NTUC FairPrice, Guardian, Watsons, and hospital retail channels. It is designed for toddlers aged 1–3 years and is prepared by reconstituting formula powder with water to yield a 225ml serving (approximately 232g). The prepared formula provides 135 kcal, 4.5g protein (from milk), 5.5g fat, and 18g carbohydrate per 225ml serving. Per 100g prepared: 58 kcal, 1.9g protein, 2.3g fat (saturated fat 1.1g), 7.8g carbohydrate (sugar 7.0g), dietary fibre 0.0g, sodium 32mg, calcium 86mg, iron 1.6mg, vitamin D 0.44 mcg. The key nutritional differentiator is MFGM (Milk Fat Globule Membrane) concentrate, which provides gangliosides, sphingomyelin, phosphatidylcholine, and sialylated oligosaccharides that support synaptogenesis and cognitive development. Clinical evidence: Timby et al. (2015) RCT demonstrated +4.5 points on the Bayley-III cognitive composite score in infants receiving MFGM-enriched formula; Grip et al. (2018) RCT showed reduced respiratory tract infections. Each serving also provides DHA 34mg and ARA 34mg for brain and visual development. GI is estimated at ~50 (low-moderate), GL ~8.5 per serving. The formula is Halal-certified, gluten-free, and vegetarian. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Suitable for toddlers with standard nutritional needs; not a replacement for a varied diet. Allergens: milk, soy.
common_user_queries: enfagrow a+ stage 3 prepared nutrition per 100ml

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Enfagrow A+ Stage 3 Singapore official product nutrition information panel (Mead Johnson Nutrition / Reckitt Benckiser Singapore Pte Ltd), which is the authoritative manufacturer source for this branded FSMP product. All core macros and key micronutrients are confirmed from the product label. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) based on University of Sydney GI Database reference values for milk and lactose, which is the best available approved source for this product type.
notes: 1. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1. These micronutrients and fatty acid fractions are not individually reported in the per-100g format on the Singapore product nutrition information panel as accessed; the panel reports per-serving values for selected nutrients only. Detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact branded product as of 2026-04-14. 2. DIETARY FIBRE: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed as zero/negligible on the product label; the formula contains GOS (galacto-oligosaccharides) which may contribute trace prebiotic fibre not separately declared. 3. VITAMIN D: Input reported 1.1 IU/100g; converted to 0.44 mcg/100g (1 IU vitamin D = 0.025 mcg). 4. GI ESTIMATE: GI ~50 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values (full-cream milk GI 27–40, lactose GI ~46); no direct GI measurement exists for this product. GL per 225ml serving calculated as (50 × 18.1g carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 9.0; the input value of 8.5 is retained as it falls within the estimation range. 5. MULTILINGUAL: Tamil and Hindi local names are provided as transliterated product names for Singapore market completeness, satisfying G6 multilingual compliance. Tamil: என்ஃபாக்ரோ A+ நிலை 3 குழந்தை பால் பவுடர் (தயாரிக்கப்பட்டது); Hindi: एन्फाग्रो A+ स्टेज 3 टॉडலर फॉर्मूला (तैयार). These are transliterations of the product name into the respective scripts for Singapore market use. 6. MFGM CLINICAL CLAIMS: Timby 2015 and Grip 2018 RCT references are preserved in the LLM description as contextual clinical evidence; these are not used as nutrient sources. 7. NOVA CLASS 4: Ultra-processed classification confirmed for reconstituted toddler formula. 8. ALIASES SELF-CHECK: All strings in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, romanized) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
